scene.description: The space won't settle. Three versions of the same corridor occupy one position — clean, flooded, collapsed — and you only see one at a time, depending where your attention rests. One object stays constant across all three: a lantern, hanging from a beam that may or may not exist. It is the anchor.

fragments.label: The Three Corridors
fragments.text: Corridor A is clean and intact. B is flooded and damaged. C is collapsed, half-voided. All occupy the same place. Looking back and forth too fast, the distinction disappears entirely.

fragments.label: The Lantern
fragments.text: Warm, unstable light, its shadows cast a half-second late. Within its small radius the walls stabilize and the contradictions quiet. Outside it, everything still shifts.

fragments.label: The Witness
fragments.text: He flickers between corridors, present only where you look. 'They think fixing the memory is the same as fixing what happened,' he says, watching the lantern, not you. 'It isn't.'

fragments.label: The Installed Lantern
fragments.text: A flash, more coherent than the rest: evacuation, distorted alarms, the lantern swinging, then a hand locking it deliberately into place. Then silence, cut clean.

fragments.label: "It's You Again"
fragments.symbol: The witness finally looks at you instead of the lantern. 'Every time you enter, you think it's the first time,' he says, very quiet. 'That's the only reason it works.' A beat. 'Oh. It's you again.'

interpretations.label: The anchor is a containment device
The anchor is a memory stabilizer
The anchor is a signal marker
The anchor is an emotional anchor — leave it unresolved
